products.productDescription: Play the game, based on the movie, based on the game! Ratchet & Clank (PS4) is a new game based on elements from the original Ratchet & Clank (PS2). Developed alongside the major motion CG-animated picture coming to theatres in 2016, Ratchet & Clank (PS4) marks the PlayStation 4 debut of PlayStation's greatest heroes. Join Ratchet, Clank, Captain Qwark and new friends as they battle to save the Solana Galaxy from the evil Chairman Drek. With an hour of new cinematics (including footage from the film), Ratchet & Clank (PS4) takes a deeper look at the characters' origin stories and modernizes the original gameplay. Explore the galaxy in a game that features new planets, new and updated gameplay segments, all-new bosses, all-new Clank gameplay, all-new flight sequences, and much more --- with completely new visuals built to demonstrate the power of the PS4! Battle your enemies with an out-of-this-world arsenal, including new weapons and fan-favorite tools of destruction from the Ratchet & Clank Future series. From the brand new Pixelizer (which transforms enemies into explosive 8-bit pixels) to franchise favorites like the dance-party-inducing Groovitron and wise-cracking robotic bodyguard Mr. Zurkon, Ratchet & Clank's arsenal has never been better. Help Ratchet and Clank save the galaxy for the first time, again, exclusively on PlayStation 4!

products.productDescription: Vesena Hearing Amplifier Aid Digital Rechargeable Personal Sound Amplifier Devices ITE for Seniors,Inner-Ear Hearing aids,2-Pack with Charging Box (White)
products.sapiensReview:
Got these for my dad and the only thing that matters is: can he hear normal conversation and the TV without cranking it up or asking people to repeat themselves. These passed that test right away. The sound boost is noticeable and it stays pretty clear/clean (not just a harsh “everything louder” sound), so he actually keeps them in.

Big win is they’re discreet in the ear. Once they’re in, you don’t really notice them and he doesn’t feel self-conscious wearing them around family. They feel comfortable for him and don’t feel bulky.

They also seem well made and the charging case makes it easy to keep them ready. After a bunch of uses they’re still working the same, no weird cutting out. Definitely recommend for seniors that need a simple, rechargeable amplifier that actually helps day to day.

products.sapiensReview:
Requires an internet connection, even when you're mainly just trying to hop into matches.

As an Evil Dead fan, the vibe is on point, but don't come in expecting a substantial story that carries you for weeks-this feels more like a multiplayer loop than a narrative-driven ride. The long-term pull will depend on whether you enjoy repeating objectives with friends and experimenting with builds.

Voice acting is a real bright spot and the playable roster variety helps keep matches from feeling identical. Controls are mostly responsive, and I didn't hit obvious technical messes. Price feels fair if you'll actually play online a lot.

products.productDescription: It's Fibbage - the lying, bluffing, fib-till-you-win multiplayer trivia party game from the makers of YOU DON'T KNOW JACK! Fool your friends with your lies, avoid theirs, and find the (outrageous) truth. And get this: your phone or tablet is your controller! It's easy. Just type in your lies to questions like: "The mayor of Rabbit Hash, Kentucky is a _____." Baby? Rabbit? Ham sandwich? It's up to you! Fool your opponents into thinking your lie is true and you'll rack up the points! But be careful, if you pick an opponent's lie, they'll be doing a Fibbage fancy dance. There's a free version that features a handful of starter questions, and you can upgrade to full Fibbage to get hours and hours of game party material! So grab your most trusting friends, family, and any other easily-fooled nitwits and get ready to laugh till you can't lie no more. Think your B.S. don't stink? You're about to find out! products.productDescription: From the Manufacturer From BioWare, the makers of Mass Effect , Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic , and Baldur's Gate comes Dragon Age: Origins . An epic tale of violence, lust, and betrayal, Dragon Age: Origins is a single player role-playing game (RPG) set in a fantasy game environment, and featuring three playable character classes, accessible in the form of three races. In addition, the game features extreme character customization, a new game engine, party-based gameplay utilizing non-player characters and a built-in personal history system for each hero character rooted in a variety of possible origin stories. .caption { font-family: Verdana, Helvetica neue, Arial, serif; font-size: 10px; font-weight: bold; font-style: italic; } ul.indent { list-style: inside disc; text-indent: -15px; } table.callout { font-family: verdana; font-size: 11px; line-height: 1. 3em; } td.vgoverview { height: 125px; background: #9DC4D8 url(https://images-na.ssl-images-amazon.com/images/G/01/electronics/detail-page/callout-bg.png) repeat-x; border-left: 1px solid #999999; border-right: 1px solid #999999; padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px; padding-bottom: 10px; width: 250px; font-family: verdana; font-size: 12px; } 6 possible hero Origin Stories. View larger . 3 classes and 3 races to play as. View larger . Gritty, brutal action. View larger . Frightening enemies and bosses. View larger . Party-based combat using NPCs. View larger . Spell combining abilities. View larger . Stunning 3D environments. View larger . Story In Dragon Age: Origins the survival of humanity rests in the hands of those chosen by fate. You are a Grey Warden, one of the last of an ancient order of guardians who have defended the lands on the continent of Thedas throughout the centuries. Betrayed by a trusted general in a critical battle, you must hunt down the traitor and bring him to justice. As you fight your way towards the final confrontation with an evil nemesis, you will face monstrous foes and engage in epic quests to unite the disparate peoples of a world at war. A romance with a seductive shapeshifter may hold the key to victory, or she may be a dangerous diversion from the heart of your mission. To be a leader, you must make ruthless decisions and be willing to sacrifice your friends and loved ones for the greater good. Gameplay Dragon Age: Origins is a 3D oriented RPG based in a dark, heroic, fantasy realm where moral choices have a lasting impression on the people you meet, the members of your own party and the world around you. The inclusion of subtitle "Origins" in the game's title refers to the six unique origin stories available to new heroes as a new game begins. Each of these has an impact on the player's motivations and his or her experience, and renders a unique prelude, path, and possible ending(s) to the game. There are many different endings to the game based on the origin story of the character and the choices you make as you play through the game. The game features three character classes--warrior, mage, and rogue--and three races of being--Human, Elf or Dwarf--that can assume these classes. Although most game elements, such as weapons, magic, etc., are available to any character, each class and race has different strengths, abilities and affinities which lend themselves to better utilizing different elements. Dragon Age: Origins is a single player game based on party-based gameplay and combat where the player can join, control and quest with up to three non-player characters (NPCs). Players can also quest alone if they so choose, but with the chance of survival are slim. Convincing NPCs to join you, and treating them well may be necessary depending on the varying sentiments between the player and the NPC, or between the NPC's in the party based on the chosen history written into the origin story accepted at the beginning of the game. This uncertainty allows for a variety of possible dynamics within the party ranging from open hostility, all the way to romance. The game progresses in real-time via a pause-and-play tactical combat system that allows the player to check inventory levels, equip a character, etc. in a slight vacuum. Additional features found in the game include: a combination of a standard loot system and a currency system based on gold silver and copper; advanced character customization functionality; the use of poison, traps and herbalism; dual-wielding skills; and "spell combos," which allow players to chain together different spells to create a unique effects. products.sapiensReview:
I’ve used a few TENS units over the years for chronic back/shoulder pain and this AVCOO one has been working really well for me so far. The 4 channels are the whole reason I bought it and it really does make day to day use easier because I don’t have to keep unplugging and moving wires around.
Setup is pretty straightforward (pads snap on, leads plug in), but I’m still learning all the icons/modes and I honestly just tried a bunch until I found what felt right. What’s been most useful for me is running my lower back and one shoulder at the same time. I usually only use 4 pads at once, but having the option to spread out more if I need it is nice.
The sensations feel smooth and controlled, not “stabby,” and I haven’t had any skin irritation from the pads. I can turn it up on my back more than I can on bony spots, so it helps that you can adjust it to what each area can handle.
For actual relief, I can feel things loosen up pretty quickly and it’s been lasting long enough that I can get through normal stuff like sitting at my desk (and even a car ride) without constantly thinking about my back. Overall it’s been an easy-to-use, comfortable TENS unit that actually does the job for me.

products.productDescription: MODREPIO Facial Hair Remover,Bikini Trimmer for Women Pubic Hair, Electric Shaver,Women Electric Razor for Legs,Nose Ear Trimmer,Hair Removal for Body,face,Bikini line20230112
products.sapiensReview:
Let me frame this up front: I’m not using this as an all-body “X-in-1” solution. I bought it mainly for quick facial peach fuzz/upper lip cleanup and occasional bikini-line touchups.

What showed up looks like the same generic handle + snap-on heads platform I’ve seen under other random brand names. The box/listing has a brand name, but I didn’t see a clear model number printed on the device itself, so don’t assume you’re buying into a well-defined “system” with interchangeable parts.

In actual use, the facial head is the only attachment I’d call consistently useful. It’s not magic, but it does a decent job taking down fine hair without having to go over the same spot a bunch of times, and it’s reasonably precise around the upper lip. The bigger body/leg style head works, but it’s slower than a real dedicated shaver and I found it easy to miss patches unless you keep the angle just right.

Power-wise, this is where the “more heads” marketing runs into physics: it’s one small motor/battery driving everything. It’s fine for quick touchups, but when you try to do a larger area it bogs down a bit and you can tell it’s not meant for long sessions. Battery life has been OK for my use (short bursts), but I wouldn’t want this as my primary shaver.

Convenience is mixed. Swapping heads is easy enough, but it also means more little parts to keep track of, and the “5-in-1” claim is mostly noise: you end up with 1-2 heads you’ll actually use and the rest are filler. If you want a cheap touch-up tool for face and maybe occasional bikini line, it’s serviceable. If you’re expecting one device to replace dedicated tools for legs/body, it won’t.

products.productDescription: NEENCE Hair Trimmer for Men, Hair Clippers for Men Zero Gapped Trimmers Professional Hair Clippers Mens Cordless Rechargeable LED T-Blade Trimmer Men's Clippers Haircut Kits Barber Grey
products.sapiensReview:
Pretty standard T-blade “zero gap” style trimmer. I grabbed it mainly for lineups/edge work (beard/neck, around ears) and it does that job well as long as you don’t expect it to replace a full-size clipper for bulk cutting.

Out of the box the blade was close to aligned (not perfectly zeroed, but close enough that I didn’t feel like loosening screws and re-centering it). It gives crisp lines without feeling like it’s chewing hair, and it hasn’t been snaggy on my neck which is usually where cheap ones show it. Build feels decent for the price too—no rattly blade assembly and the body doesn’t feel like it’ll crack the first time it gets bumped in a drawer.

Power-wise it’s been solid so far. I’m not babying the battery and I’m getting multiple cleanup sessions before I even think about charging again, and the LED display helps avoid the “dies mid-lineup” issue. Charging is USB-based which is what I want since I can top it off from basically anything I already have (phone bricks, car charger, laptop, power banks, power stations, etc.). Only downside on the “current gen” ladder is the port: this is still not USB-C, which is annoying in 2026 because it means keeping yet another cable around.

Overall: good precision trimmer with reliable power and an actually useful display, just wish it would join the USB-C world.

products.productDescription: NEENCE Hair Trimmer for Men, Hair Clippers for Men Zero Gapped Trimmers Professional Hair Clippers Mens Cordless Rechargeable LED T-Blade Trimmer Men's Clippers Haircut Kits Barber Grey
products.sapiensReview:
This is one of those cordless “T-blade / zero-gapped” trimmers that show up under a bunch of brand names, so I treated it as a generic budget platform and bought it mainly for edging (neckline/sideburns) and quick beard line cleanup, not as a full-on head hair clipper.

Out of the box it feels like mid-tier budget hardware: not toy-cheap, but also not Wahl/Andis-level in machining and finish. The listing leans hard on “zero gapped,” but what you actually get is a T-blade trimmer with an adjustable blade alignment. Mine arrived close, but not perfectly aligned, and you’ll want to check the corners before you run it into skin.

The controls are basically one real state: on/off. The LED display is useful for battery % (at least as an indicator), but it doesn’t turn this into a more capable tool. Power-wise it’s fine for daily stubble and edging; it will cut clean lines if you go slow. Where it starts to bog is trying to do larger bulk removal like a clipper—same motor, different expectations.

The main design risk on these kits is always the attachment/guard retention. The guards here are friction-fit; they stay on, but I don’t trust them the way I trust a positive-lock system. If you’re heavy-handed or you’re swapping guards a lot, this style is more likely to loosen over time and give you a surprise shorter cut.

For my use (detail work and touch-ups) it’s a decent value, but I’d still tell most people: don’t buy this thinking the extra “professional kit” vibe changes the underlying class of tool. If you need consistent guard cuts on head hair, spend for a real clipper platform.

products.productDescription: 9-in-1 Head Shavers for Bald Men w/ 7 Floating Heads, LED Display, USB Rechargeable, Mens Grooming Kit w/ Rotary Shaver, Nose Trimmer, Hair Clipper, Brush. Cordless Electric Razor Wet and Dry, Gold
products.sapiensReview:
This is the newer style head shaver kit (7 floating heads / “7D”) vs the older 5/6 head models, and that alone makes it easier to get a consistent pass around the crown/neck without constantly changing angles. The LED % display is the better implementation too (not the vague 3-light dots), so you can actually plan a shave instead of guessing.

Charging is plain USB, which I prefer because it plays nice with my existing ecosystem: phone bricks, laptop ports, car USB, power banks, and even power stations/solar USB outputs when traveling. Mine did include a power block, which is rare in this category and honestly changes the value because it’s ready to run out of the box (no scavenger hunt for a spare adapter). If the cable is a standard USB cable you can replace anywhere, that’s a big win vs the sets that use a weird/proprietary lead.

Build feels solid for the price and the head assembly hasn’t felt flimsy. Battery has been good so far with the % readout; I’m not seeing weird drain or surprise shutoffs. Accessories are the usual mix: the rotary head is the main reason to buy it, and the brush is actually useful if you pair it with a face wash, while some of the other add-ons feel more like “included because 9‑in‑1” than must-haves.

Overall, if you catch it at a reasonable coupon price, it’s a good value 7D kit that’s easy to live with.

products.productDescription: 1875 Watt Negative Ionic Hair Dryer, ENISECA Precise Wind Heat/Speed Setting Tech Professional Salon Blow Dryer Fast Drying Low Noise with Powerful AC Motor and Diffuser Comb for Women Men, Grey
products.sapiensReview:
I wanted to like this ENISECA 1875W “ionic” dryer because the motor does feel strong and it will move water off the hair quickly when you can get the settings you need. My normal routine on fine hair is very specific: I need low wind with a hotter heat (so I’m not blasting my scalp and hairline around, but I can still get the cuticle to lay down and be done in one controlled pass). That combo is basically not available here. The heat/speed feel paired in a way that keeps forcing me into either higher wind than I want, or not enough heat to finish neatly, which means I end up chasing flyaways and frizz instead of completing my usual 6–8 minute routine.

What really bothered me is that the brand leans into the idea that the lower speed is “for kids/beginners.” Adults with fine, fragile, color-treated, or sensitive-scalp hair often need low airflow with real heat. Calling that a “kid” setting feels like an excuse for a limited control design, and for me that’s a trust issue because the controls are the product.

Then the dealbreaker: mine crapped out and went dead mid-use. Not a “let it cool and it comes back” situation—just stopped and would not restart. A dryer can be loud or heavy and I’ll work around it, but it can’t die in the middle of my routine.

If you only ever use the preset pairings and you get a good unit, you may find it dries fast, but anyone who needs specific heat/wind combinations (and anyone who can’t tolerate a reliability gamble) should pass.

products.productDescription: Ionic Hair Dryer, VLISBO 1875W Hair Blow Dryer Intelligent Temperature Heat & Wind Speed Settings Technology, Negative Ion Hairdryer with Powerful AC Motor for Hair Care with Diffuser for Home, Travel
products.sapiensReview:
This is a perfectly serviceable 1875W-class ionic hair dryer, but don’t buy it expecting anything genuinely “intelligent.” In my use it lands about where a mid-range Conair/Revlon AC-motor dryer lands: strong airflow, heats up quickly, and it’ll dry normal hair without having to live at max heat the whole time.

The controls are simple, but the “smart” part feels more like marketing than behavior you can rely on. The dryer doesn’t consistently remember where you left it, so you end up doing the same little button/switch dance each time you pick it up. That sounds minor, but it’s the kind of repeatable annoyance that shows up every single session.

Also, the temperature readout is Celsius-only. If you’re selling this into the US market, that’s not a “preference” thing—it’s just a weird design miss, because it makes the display a lot less useful for quick decisions.

Build-wise it’s decent for the price: the body doesn’t feel like it’s going to crack the first time it gets knocked around, and the motor/airflow combination feels like an actual AC dryer (not the weak DC ones). The attachments work, but the retention is the part I don’t love—there are small plastic clips/tabs living right in the hot airflow path. They hold fine now, but that’s exactly the kind of mechanism that gets brittle/loose with heat cycling and repeated swaps. My “ownership plan” is basically: pick the nozzle or diffuser you use most and leave it on, rather than constantly changing them.

Noise and balance are reasonable. It’s not whisper-quiet, but it’s not the screechy cheap-dryer sound either, and it’s comfortable enough to hold through a normal dry without feeling like a brick.

products.productDescription: Topretty Electric Head Shaver for Men, 5 in 1 Skull Shaver, 8-Head Upgrade Wet Dry Beard Trimmer, Rotary Razor Shaving Kit with Floating Head, Bald Grooming Kit with Hair&Nose Trimmer, Massager, Brush
products.sapiensReview:
Let me note upfront that I’m reviewing this primarily as a head shaver (very short hair / “bald-ish”), with some use on the face/neck to see if it can replace a basic rotary shaver. This is one of those many Amazon “multi head” skull shavers, except this one is the 8-head style and comes as a 5-in-1 kit (head shaver, trimmer, nose trimmer, brush, massager). The general idea is good, but as usual with these, the real question is whether the underlying motor platform can actually drive that many cutters without you paying for extra head-count theater.

On my head it does a respectable job in wet/dry use, but the 8-head setup doesn’t feel like it’s buying you much besides a bigger contact patch. With these shavers, more heads usually just means more load on what is likely the same small motor you see in the 5/6/7-head variants. My rule of thumb after trying a few of these: if you’re choosing between similar-looking models, I’d rather have fewer heads that stay moving with less drag than a higher head count that can bog down on denser areas.

For face/neck, it’s “okay” but not a true replacement for a decent 3-head Norelco-style shaver. It can get you presentable, but it’s bulkier to maneuver and it’s easier to miss spots around the jawline.

The biggest ownership issue for me is cleaning. The hair capture area is small, and the center section doesn’t just pop open in a satisfying way—you end up doing the same twist/pull routine every time to get hair out of the middle. That’s the kind of repeated chore that will decide whether you still use it a month from now.

Also, I don’t see a clear path to replacing just the cutters/inner blades. Everything points to “replace the whole 8-head assembly” when it wears, which makes this feel disposable even if it works fine today.

As for the 5-in-1 attachments: the clipper/trimmer head is the only one I’d call genuinely useful, and it’s about as good as a basic dedicated detail trimmer. The nose hair head is the usual tiny rotary type (fine in a pinch), and the brush/massager are the kind of add-ons that will end up in a drawer.

One other note: this looks like the same general OEM platform as a bunch of other no-name 8-head kits on Amazon (same head geometry and attachment interface), so I’d treat it as “pick the seller/price/return policy” rather than assuming this brand is meaningfully different unless you can confirm replacement heads actually exist for this exact mount.

products.productDescription: AW(AidallsWellup) Head Shaver, AW Men’s 5-in-1 Electric Head Shavers for Bald Men, Head Shavers for Men, Anti-Pinch, Ergonomic Design, Cordless, and Rechargeable.
products.sapiensReview:
Let me note up front that I’m not bald, so I’m not using this as an every-other-day full scalp shaver. I bought it mostly as a compact, rechargeable “cleanup” tool (neckline, around the ears) and to see if any of the 5‑in‑1 attachments were more than bundle filler.

This is one of those products where the marketing (“more heads/more functions”) is doing a lot of the work. The core shaver is a multi-rotary head, and the basic physics still apply: you don’t magically get more motor/torque just because you bolt on more cutters. On lighter stubble it does OK, but if you push it into denser areas the head can bog a bit and you end up making more passes than you’d expect. That’s the tradeoff with these high-head-count designs: coverage looks good on paper, but torque-per-cutter and control feel are what you live with.

The bigger issue for me is maintenance. Hair capture is limited and the debris path is fussy; after a couple uses it was obvious this is the kind of shaver that demands cleaning basically every time if you don’t want it to start feeling worse. The center area in particular is annoying to get fully cleared out without fiddling, and that’s the sort of small daily tax that makes me stop reaching for a device.

On the “5‑in‑1” part: a couple attachments are usable for quick touch-ups, but most of them feel like generic add-ons that don’t perform like their dedicated equivalents. Also, the way some of the attachments lock on (small plastic tabs/retainers) doesn’t inspire confidence for long-term swapping on/off; it’s the kind of retention design that tends to loosen or snap before the motor dies.

The other long-term concern is replaceability. I don’t see a clear path to replacing individual cutters/rotary rings as wear items (the thing that actually dulls). If the head assembly is the only realistic replacement, this turns into a semi-disposable tool.

If you want a cheap, occasional-use gadget and you’re willing to baby the cleaning, it can work. If you’re expecting a durable, maintainable “main” shaver because it says 5‑in‑1, I think you’ll be disappointed after the return window closes.

products.productDescription: For ages 13 and up only. *Offer valid with the purchase of a new Meta Quest 2 device (128GB or 256GB) ("Qualifying Product") between August 1, 2022 12:01 AM PST- December 31, 2022 11:59 PM PST and while supplies last. User account ("Account") required. Offer Item (digital content): Beat Saber in the Meta Quest Store (approx. retail value $29.99). Additional downloadable content, including future music packs and other content, sold separately. To receive Offer Item, Qualifying Product must be first activated between August 1, 2022 12:01 AM PST - January 31, 2023 11:59 PM PST through an Account without Beat Saber entitlement previously enabled on such account ("Activation"). Upon Activation, you will receive a notification message via email, in your mobile app, and in VR with a link to redeem the Offer Item from the Meta Quest Store. There are no redemption codes in connection with this Offer. Offer Item must be redeemed within 14 days upon Activation. Once redeemed, Offer Item remains available for download indefinitely. Limit of one (1) Offer Item per Qualifying Product, regardless of the number of Accounts connected to such Qualifying Product. Qualifying Products first activated through an Account with Beat Saber entitlement previously enabled are ineligible to receive Offer Item. Not valid on prior orders or purchases. Offer is non-transferable, not for resale, and not valid for cash or cash equivalent. Offer may be canceled or modified at any time without notice. Void where prohibited or restricted.
products.sapiensReview:
This listing is basically the Beat Saber promo tied to buying/activating a new Meta Quest 2, so just know what you're actually paying for. On the Quest 2 side, the key specs are still the same: 1832×1920 per eye at up to 120Hz, and the lenses are decent once you take the time to dial in IPD and strap fit. Clarity is good in the sweet spot for rhythm games like Beat Saber, but it's not "edge-to-edge" sharp and you can get some glare if your fit is off.

Comfort is the make-or-break thing for me. The stock strap is fine for short sessions, but for anything longer I ended up needing a better strap (even a cheap halo style) and a different face pad to reduce pressure on my cheeks/forehead. Once I did that, Beat Saber sessions were way more enjoyable and the headset felt less front-heavy.

PC link is still a huge value multiplier if you have a gaming PC. Wired Link works reliably with a good cable, and Air Link can be great if your Wi‑Fi is solid, but it's more sensitive to your network than people admit. Having PCVR access is what keeps the Quest 2 from feeling like a closed toy, even though you're still dealing with Meta's ecosystem.

The promo itself is straightforward (no code), but the fine print is strict: you need a Meta account, you have to activate in the window, and you only get it if your account didn't already own Beat Saber. Also, don't forget the "additional music packs sold separately" part-Beat Saber can turn into ongoing DLC spend if you want specific tracks.

Overall, if you were already buying a new Quest 2, getting Beat Saber included is solid value. If you're trying to buy this as "just the game," this isn't that, and the account/eligibility restrictions are the main reason I wouldn't recommend it blindly.

products.sapiensReview:
This is the Shallmu 6-blade head shaver kit (6-in-1). Overall it’s a pretty standard 6D style unit, and in 2026 that already feels a step behind the 7D heads that are becoming the norm at the same $40-ish price band.

Shave quality on a bald head is solid for the price: it gets close without having to do a million passes. My bigger concern is the touch switch—when it works it’s fine, but touch controls on these can be finicky and I’d rather have a reliable click button than something that occasionally doesn’t register.

Also worth noting: the listing should be clearer on the exact water rating (IPX level) and the charging standard. If it’s not USB-C and doesn’t include a power brick, that’s hidden friction/cost versus newer kits. The extra heads are mixed usefulness (the brush is the only one I consistently use), so for me this only makes sense if you get it on a coupon deal.
